title: artemisia arbuscula subsp. longicaulis
intro: Artemisia arbuscula subsp. longicaulis is a subspecies of the artemisia arbuscula plant, commonly known as the dwarf wormwood or long-stemmed artemisia. This shrubby perennial plant is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Artemisia arbuscula subsp. longicaulis has been found to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system.
